label: North Island · Hiking
title: Nordby Bakker & Issehoved
items: Route A (18km loop): Nordby → Nordby Church → Møgelskår → west coast cliffs → Ballebjerg (64m lookout) → Pelkeshøj → Issehoved (northernmost tip) → east coast beach → Nordby
Route B (11km): Ballebjerg → Issehoved → east coast beach → Nordby
Trailhead: Nordby village — ~16km by e-bike from Yduns Have via Sælvig

label: South Island · Hiking
title: Brattingsborg Forest & Vesborg Fyr
items: Brattingsborg Skov: forest streams, fallow deer (best at dawn/dusk), pheasants and squirrels. Trail follows coast to Lushage lighthouse
Vesborg Fyr: 1858 white lighthouse with panoramic views to Funen, Zealand and Jutland. Medieval castle ruins of Valdemar Atterdag on site. Entry DKK 30 cash
Southern coastal path: Ørby → Nørreskifte beach (bathing jetty, sea views). Forest open to walkers and cyclists year-round

label: Cycling · South Loop
title: South Island Cycling
items: ~35km loop: Yduns Have → Tranebjerg → Pillemark → Tranemosen (12-acre wetland, birdlife) → Kolby (medieval church) → Vesborg Fyr → Brattingsborg → Ørby → Nørreskifte → Ballen → Tranebjerg
Stops: Samsø Museum in Tranebjerg, farm roadside stalls (asparagus in May), Røgeriet lunch at Ballen harbour
Bikes booked with Samsø Cykel — pickup at Sælvig harbour on arrival

label: Water · Stavns Fjord
title: Kayak & SUP — Stavns Fjord
items: SamSurf at Ballen Strandcamping: stable sit-on-top kayaks and SUP boards, no experience needed (~DKK 150–250/person)
Paddle Stavns Fjord Natura 2000 reserve: shallow water (30–40cm), 2,000+ cormorant pairs, gray herons, eider ducks, seals on the islets
Note: Besser Rev sandbar closed April 1–July 15 for breeding birds — the fjord itself is open

label: Nordby · Nature
title: Samsø Labyrint
items: World's largest permanent tree labyrinth — 60,000 m² planted in willow, oak and apple. 5,380m of paths
Near Nordby at M.B.Jensensvej 12 — combine with the Nordby Bakker hiking day. Open May–September

label: Restaurants · May
title: Food & Drink
items: Skipperly (Ballen harbour) — #1 on TripAdvisor Samsø. Daily fish from harbour + vegetables from island farms. Terrace overlooking marina. Book: +45 86 59 10 18
Restaurant Behrnt at Ballen Badehotel — Danish country cooking with French inspiration since 1860. Local Samsø ingredients, warm bathhouse atmosphere
Røgeriet (Ballen harbour) — smoked fish, fish cakes and freshly baked bread. Eat by the pier or takeaway. Havnevej 3
Samsø Madsnedkeri (Tranebjerg) — converted barn, local produce, fish & chips, seasonal street food. DKK 89–250. Book: 51 91 19 57
Nordby Kro (Nordby) — classic inn kitchen, seasonal menu with modern twist on local classics. Pairs well with the north island hiking day
Søster Sidevind (Besser) — local favourite café, smørrebrød and fresh ingredients
Farm stalls island-wide — asparagus peak in May, early strawberries, rhubarb, fresh herbs
